title: Hello World
description:
Write a program that prints **Hello, World!** to the screen.

This is the classic first program in any language!
hints: Use the print() function
Put your text inside quotes: print("Hello, World!")
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Print "Hello, World!" to the screen

title: Greet the User
description:
Write a program that reads a **name** from input and prints a greeting in the format:
**Hello, {name}!**
hints: Use input() to read the name
Use an f-string: f"Hello, {name}!"
Or use concatenation: "Hello, " + name + "!"
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a name and print a greeting

title: Simple Calculator
description: Read two integers from input (one per line) and print their **sum**, **difference**, and **product**, each on a new line.
hints: Use int(input()) to read numbers
Store them in variables a and b
Print a+b, a-b, and a*b on separate lines
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read two numbers and print sum, difference, product

title: Even or Odd
description: Read an integer from input and print **"Even"** if it's even, or **"Odd"** if it's odd.
hints: Use the modulo operator: number % 2
If the remainder is 0, the number is even
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a number and print Even or Odd

title: Grade Calculator
description:
Read a score (0–100) from input and print the grade:
- **90–100**: A
- **80–89**: B
- **70–79**: C
- **60–69**: D
- **Below 60**: F
hints: Use if/elif/else chain
Check from highest to lowest: if score >= 90, elif score >= 80, etc.
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a score and print the letter grade

title: FizzBuzz
description:
Read a number **N** and print the numbers from 1 to N, but:
- For multiples of 3, print **"Fizz"**
- For multiples of 5, print **"Buzz"**
- For multiples of both 3 and 5, print **"FizzBuzz"**
hints: Use a for loop with range(1, n+1)
Check divisibility by 15 first (both 3 and 5)
Then check 3, then 5, then just print the number

examples.output:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz

testCases.expectedOutput:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz

starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read N and print FizzBuzz from 1 to N

title: Sum of Even Numbers
description:
Read a number **N**, then read **N** integers, and print the **sum of all even numbers** among them.

**Hint:** Use the modulo operator `%` to check if a number is even.
hints: Initialize a sum variable to 0
Loop N times, reading a number each time
If number % 2 == 0, add it to the sum
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read N, then read N numbers
# Print the sum of even numbers

title: Collect Names
description:
Write a program that keeps asking the user to enter names. When the user types **"show"**, the program should stop asking and print all the names entered so far, each on a new line.

**Hint:** Use a `while` loop and `input()` function.
hints: Use a while True loop
Read input each iteration
If input is 'show', break out of the loop
Otherwise, append to a list, then print with '\n'.join()
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Keep asking for names until 'show' is entered
# Then print all the names

title: Multiplication Table
description:
Read a number **N** and print its multiplication table from 1 to 10.

Format each line as: **N x i = result**
hints: Use a for loop with range(1, 11)
Use an f-string: f"{n} x {i} = {n*i}"
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read N and print its multiplication table

title: Reverse a String
description:
Read a string from input and print the **reversed version** of that string.

**Hint:** Python strings support slicing with a step value.
hints: You can reverse a string with slicing: s[::-1]
Or use a loop to build the reversed string
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a string and print it reversed

title: Count Vowels
description:
Read a string and print how many **vowels** (a, e, i, o, u) it contains.

Count both uppercase and lowercase vowels.
hints: Convert the string to lowercase first
Loop through each character
Check if it's in 'aeiou'
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a string and count the vowels

title: Palindrome Check
description: Read a string and print **"Yes"** if it reads the same forwards and backwards (case-insensitive), otherwise print **"No"**.
hints: Convert to lowercase first
Compare the string with its reverse
s.lower() == s.lower()[::-1]
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a string and check if it's a palindrome

title: Find the Maximum
description:
Read **N**, then read **N** integers. Print the **largest number** among them.

Do **not** use the built-in `max()` function.
hints: Read all numbers into a list
Set the first number as the current max
Loop through the rest, update max if you find a larger one
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read N numbers and find the largest (no max())

title: Remove Duplicates
description: Read **N**, then read **N** integers. Print the unique numbers in the **order they first appeared**, each on a new line.
hints: Use a list to track seen numbers
Only add a number if it's not already in the seen list
Print each unique number
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read N numbers and print unique ones in order

title: Factorial
description:
Read a non-negative integer **N** and print its **factorial** (N!).

Recall: 0! = 1, and N! = 1 × 2 × 3 × ... × N
hints: Define a function factorial(n)
Use a loop or recursion
Don't forget: 0! = 1
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Define a factorial function and use it

title: Is Prime?
description:
Read an integer **N** (N ≥ 2) and print **"Yes"** if it's a prime number, otherwise **"No"**.

A prime number is only divisible by 1 and itself.
hints: Check divisibility from 2 to sqrt(N)
If any number divides N evenly, it's not prime
Import math and use math.isqrt(n) for the square root
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Check if N is a prime number

title: Fibonacci Sequence
description:
Read an integer **N** and print the first **N** numbers of the Fibonacci sequence, each on a new line.

The Fibonacci sequence starts: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ...
hints: Start with a=0 and b=1
In each step: print a, then set a, b = b, a+b
Repeat N times
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Print the first N Fibonacci numbers
